reviews.review_msg: Amazing

She is a kind prof but there was only like 3 aspects where we were graded on: Midterm, Final, and Labs. Barely went to lectures and still did alright. There is no textbook she just relies on slides. Labs were also interesting. Midterms are fair.
The class was stress free. Easy to communicate with the instrucor. I enjoyed the class it relates to the real world.
good prog
He delivered the materials in a nice and simple way. He was so helpful during lab tutorials.

Makes mistakes in lecture, accent can be difficult, just reads his slides. He's not responsive to feedback and answers without understanding your question (akes you feel stupid for asking). Midterm was writing intensive, took the entire time period, and required more than was covered in lecture. Lecture material was easy. Labs okay but unclear.

Grad student. Cannot communicate effectively, so course content, interaction, and student learning suffer.  Take equivalent BISC 204 instead, or wait for a non-sessional instructor. He means well, but he is just not adequately responsive to questions/discussions (lacks communication skills). Disappointing and substandard for university.

This prof's class is very challenging. Exams are extremely heavy and expect a deep understanding, so cramming won't save you. Grading is very strict with barely any partial credit. Lectures are dry and mostly just reading off slides with little engagement. The class average was around a C+, which says a lot, just be ready to put in a lot of effort.
Ian rocks

What a sweet guy. Very organized with his lectures, slides were printed out in a book for us to purchase. Explained everything really well at a level we could understand.  However, hes very much a 'grandpa' and having lectures at 10:30 on a friday make class very sleep worthy. Make sure to drink your coffee!! No midterm and easy/fun term project!
Very organized. Lectures flow and he covers a lot of material. Marks are split fairly well. Enjoys the subject material and is willing to share his knowledge. Clear and concise.
Remember bring tons of paper for his lecture, no overhead, tough exam
Very clear and organized, interesting lectures, English accent

Great class on overcoming poor administration/course formatting challenges. The actual geog classwork is trivial.

Assignments are posted on random days and due on random days. The syllabus is meaningless. No consistency.

My best advice, and this is NOT an exaggeration: check your assignments page LITERALLY EVERY DAY or you will miss assignments.

She has been one of my favorite instructors that I have ever taken. She is always very empathetic with her students, very inspirational, and genuinely wants them to excel in her class and in life overall. The only critique I would have for her is to make her lectures more engaging, but she is a fantastic teacher and person.

Great Prof who clearly wants you to do well. Class average was like A-, and I appreciate that she kept the grades as they were. Really kind too and would often post job suggestions, unfortunately I wasn't looking to go into that field.

Very nice person, so passionate on her work. Sometimes love to share own stuff in announcement. 
She has always asked proactively about potential questions or concerns during lab sections. 

Be aware of labs, presentation, and of course exams.

Dr. Pearson is passionate about indigenous peoples and making the world a better place. She is a genuinely kind person. Her lectures were hard to follow at times, I often didn't know what to review from class. I found her grading was quite lenient, however the TAs were not. The class was not very organized either, no strict rubrics or syllabus.
Really nice and cares about first nations a lot. I would take again

She's a nice person but a really bad public speaker, and rambles on about irrelevant topics during the lecture such as the Nazis and how her family fought in the war. The way she presented her lectures made it so that it was easier to understand when she wasn't speaking vs when she was.
